titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 618

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=818 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 14:52:15.728949+00 | translation moa=819 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 14:52:15.738801+00

div.p:
Alex’ Dei Gratia Rex Scott’. Episcopis . Abbatibus . Comitibus . Baronibus . Iusticiariis . vicecomitibus . prepositis .
Ministris . et omnibus aliis probis hominibus tocius terre sue salutem . Sciant presentes et futuri nos diuine pie
tatis Intuitu dedisse . concessisse . et hac carta nostra confirmasse deo et Beate Marie et sancto Chutberto et
Thome priori de Coldingham’ . et Monachis Ibidem deo seruientibus et in perpetuum seruituris illas viginti
Marchas annuas quas nos et antecessores nostri percipere consueuimus de Coldinghamsshyre nomine waytinge. Tenendas
percipiendas . et habendas . eisdem Monachis et successoribus suis in perpetuum . In liberam puram et perpetuam elemosinam
adeo libere . quiete . plenarie . et honorifice . sicut Idem Monachi aliquas elemosinas . liberius . quietius . plenius . et
honorificentius . tenent et possident . Testibus . A . abbate de Melros . W. de Bondington Cancellario . Waltero
filio Alani Senescallo Iusticiario scoc’ . Patricio filio Comitis Patricii . Iohanne de Maccuswel Camerario . Rogero
Avenel . Dauid Marscallo . Eymer’ de Maccuswel . Iohanne de Striuelin’ . apud Berwyk’ Sexto
decimo die Maii . Anno regni domini Regis octauodecimo .

p.cl: Alexander by the grace of God, to bishops, abbots, earls, barons, justiciars, sheriffs, provosts, officials, and all other worthy men of the whole of his land sends greeting.

Let present and future know us, moved by God's love, to have given, granted, and by this our charter to have made firm to God and Blessed Mary and Saint Cuthbert and Thomas the prior of Coldingham, and to the monks in that place serving God now and for ever in the future, the twenty annual merks which we and our ancestors were accustomed to receive from Coldinghamshire as waiting. For the same monks and their successors for ever to have, receive, and hold, in free, pure and perpetual alms, as freely, quitely, fully, and honourably as the same monks freely, quitely, fully, and honourably hold and possess any alms.

As witnesses, Adam, abbot of Melrose, William of Bondington, chancellor, Walter son of Alan, steward, justiciar of Scotia, Patrick son of Earl Patrick, John of Maxwell, chamberlain, Roger Avenel, David Marshall, Eymer of Maxwell, John of Stirling,
at Berwick on the sixteenth day of May, in the eighteenth year of the lord king's reign [1232].
